mainText:
Good morning. Here are your headlines for Tuesday 26 May 2026.

AI models and products. May 2026 delivered more frontier-model launches in a single month than any prior period with Gemini 3.5 Flash, Composer 2.5, Grok Build 0.1, and Anthropic self-hosted sandboxes among others landing within 22 calendar days.

UK personal finance. Trading 212 offers top easy-access ISA rate of 4.62% for new money.

Israeli politics. 110 of 120 lawmakers voted to advance a bill dissolving parliament, setting up possible early elections by September over ultra-Orthodox military draft conflict.

UK politics. Labour lost more than 1,100 council seats while Reform UK gained over 1,400 seats in May elections.

Sleep and wearables. Fitbit Air ships today at 99 pounds, directly challenging Whoop's subscription model.

B2B SaaS and go-to-market. Corgi, an AI-native insurance platform, raised 160 million dollars in Series B funding at a 1.3 billion dollar valuation on May 6.

Today's question: With agentic AI pricing shifting away from seat-based models, are you advising your SaaS clients to restructure their sales comp before consumption-based deals become the market standard, or are you still seeing resistance from traditional sales leadership?
fullText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Tuesday 26 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products
- Gemini 3.5 Flash reached general availability at $1.50 input and $9 output per million tokens with 76.2% on Terminal-Bench 2.1, beating Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agents.
- Gemini 3.5 Flash at $1.50/$9.00, Composer 2.5 Standard at $0.50/$2.50, and Grok Build at $1.00/$2.00 represent a new pricing tier for frontier-class coding and agent intelligence with sub-$1.00 per million token input now available from production-grade models.
- Gemini Omni can create video from any input representing a leap forward in multimodality, while Google Antigravity has moved beyond AI tools that help write to agents that help act with agentic experiences now unlocked across Google products.

💷 UK personal finance
- Best one-year fixed ISA rates stand at 4.70% AER and two-year fixes at 4.71% AER as of 22 May 2026.
- Major mortgage lenders including Nationwide, HSBC, Halifax and Santander have been cutting mortgage rates in May 2026, with First Direct offering the best five-year fix at 4.48% for house purchases.
- The Bank of England held rates at 3.75% on 30 April 2026 with a vote of 8 to 1, with one member preferring a 0.25 percentage point increase to 4%, and the next decision is scheduled for Thursday 18 June 2026.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics
- Netanyahu's coalition may collapse after ultra-Orthodox party Degel HaTorah called for parliament dissolution over military draft exemptions, with a final election potentially triggered within 90 days if approved.
- Opposition polls show Netanyahu's right-wing bloc at 50 seats versus centrist opposition parties at 60 seats out of 120, with Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid leading the opposition challenge.
- Yair Lapid called the emerging US-Iran nuclear deal a "disaster," saying the regime grew stronger and the agreement fails to address ballistic missile threats.

🇬🇧 UK politics
- Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces resignation calls from Labour MPs after the defeats, though Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned on 14 May citing lost confidence in his leadership.
- Reform's Nigel Farage took control of Havering, his party's first London local authority, plus Essex county council and Sunderland.
- Labour lost control of two London councils, Lambeth and Lewisham, both switching to Green Party control.

😴 Sleep and wearables
- Fitbit Air launches today May 26 2026 at £84.99 with no mandatory subscription, versus Whoop at 199 to 359 pounds annually.
- Sleepal AI Lamp crowdfunding launched in May 2026 with 1022-night validation study using polysomnography comparison, setting a new standard for contactless sleep monitoring versus consumer trackers with minimal validation.
- HRV-based sleep staging now achieves substantial agreement with polysomnography lab data, reaching agreement levels of 0.72 for REM and deep sleep classification from wearable sensors.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market
- Enter, a São Paulo-based legal AI platform for mass litigation, raised over 100 million dollars in Series B at approximately 1.2 billion dollar valuation, becoming Latin America's first AI unicorn.
- The AI for sales and marketing market is projected to grow from 58 billion dollars in 2025 to over 240 billion dollars by 2030, with 71 percent of B2B sales executives increasing AI spending in 2024 alone.
- Traditional SaaS incentive compensation based on annual contract value is being upended as agentic AI pricing shifts from seat licenses to consumption and results-based factors, with companies evaluating hybrid incentives or consumption-only models.

Today's question: With agentic AI pricing shifting away from seat-based models, are you advising your SaaS clients to restructure their sales comp before consumption-based deals become the market standard, or are you still seeing resistance from traditional sales leadership?
